all_ranked.name: Chambers Cogeneration LP
all_ranked.notes: 285MW retired coal plant in Salem County NJ that closed May 2022. Unlike sister plant Logan which was demolished, Chambers structures remain intact. Strong transmission infrastructure, adjacent to Chemours chemical complex, confirmed battery storage redevelopment plans indicate viable grid connection and regulatory approval pathway.
all_ranked.acquirability_notes: Joint ownership between Starwood Energy (60%) and Atlantic Power (40%) with committed battery storage redevelopment plans but no active demolition yet
all_ranked.current_owner: Starwood Energy Group Global LLC (60%), Atlantic Power Corporation (40%)
all_ranked.redevelopment_plans: Battery energy storage facility development as part of $1 billion joint venture with Energy Management Inc to store renewable energy
all_ranked.partnership_path: Approach Starwood Energy Group directly as they are leading the battery storage redevelopment, or potentially work with both Starwood and Atlantic Power given joint ownership structure

all_ranked.name: Pedricktown Cogeneration Company LP

all_ranked.name: B L England

all_ranked.name: PSEG Essex Generating Station
all_ranked.notes: Plant closed in 2022 after transitioning from coal to gas peaking facility. Major environmental contamination contributor to Diamond Alkali Superfund site on Passaic River. Located in environmental justice community with persistent air quality issues. Switch house remains but main turbine hall was demolished. Site has documented history of kerosene leaks, oil spills, and regulatory violations dating to 1970s.
all_ranked.acquirability_notes: Site remains owned by PSEG with extensive environmental contamination history and ongoing Superfund liabilities. Major environmental justice concerns in Ironbound neighborhood. Property appears abandoned but still under utility control with active environmental monitoring obligations.
all_ranked.partnership_path: Direct negotiation with PSEG would be required, but significant environmental remediation costs and Superfund site proximity make acquisition challenging. Would need extensive environmental due diligence given plant's contribution to Passaic River contamination and ongoing EPA oversight.

all_ranked.name: Bayonne Plant Holding LLC

all_ranked.name: Century 21 - Distribution & Corporate Offices

all_ranked.name: Mickleton Station
all_ranked.notes: 71.2 MW retired natural gas combustion turbine. Constellation completed $16.4B Calpine acquisition Jan 2026, creating largest US power producer focused on AI/data center market. Site generated only 68.2 MWh in Q4 2023 before retirement, indicating minimal recent operation. Located in Gloucester County with existing transmission infrastructure.
all_ranked.acquirability_notes: Constellation Energy (following Calpine acquisition in Jan 2026) is actively focusing on data center partnerships and divesting non-core assets. Small retired peaker plants may be available for redevelopment.
all_ranked.partnership_path: Approach Constellation Energy directly regarding retired asset disposition. Company is actively monetizing power infrastructure for data center market and may consider site partnerships or sales for smaller non-core assets.

all_ranked.name: Walmart Fulfillment Center
all_ranked.notes: One of two Pedricktown facilities closed as part of consolidation to next-generation facilities

all_ranked.name: Parlin Power Plant
all_ranked.notes: Status discrepancy: Global Energy Monitor shows as operating (last updated Jan 2026) while provided data indicates retired. Plant has excellent data center characteristics - 135MW capacity, natural gas infrastructure, direct 500kV transmission access via Neptune Cable system to Long Island, strategic location in Sayreville industrial corridor. Site near major transmission infrastructure including converter station for undersea cable to Long Island.
all_ranked.acquirability_notes: Major utility owner with established infrastructure, but recent Global Energy Monitor data shows as operating, creating uncertainty about actual retirement status
all_ranked.partnership_path: Direct approach to Con Edison corporate development team or explore joint venture opportunities through their energy transition initiatives. Con Edison has divested other power plants and may be open to site partnerships for data center development given their grid modernization focus.

all_ranked.name: Carlls Corner
all_ranked.notes: Small natural gas peaker plant with 2 generators. Constellation completed Calpine acquisition in January 2026, creating nation's largest power producer focused on AI data center market. Plant location in rural Cumberland County near NJ Routes 77/56 intersection provides highway access. Love Lane Solar farm also operates in same area, indicating grid connectivity.
all_ranked.acquirability_notes: Now owned by Constellation Energy through Calpine acquisition. Plant shows minimal recent generation (43.9 MWh Q3-Q4 2023), suggesting it may be mothballed or retired. Corporate ownership may be more open to divestiture given focus on larger strategic assets.
all_ranked.current_owner: Constellation Energy Corporation (through Calpine New Jersey Generation LLC)
all_ranked.partnership_path: Contact Constellation Energy's commercial development team. With their focus on AI data centers and the recent $26.6B Calpine acquisition integration, they may be divesting non-strategic peaker assets. The site's rural New Jersey location and small 83.8 MW capacity may not align with their data center co-location strategy.

top_10.name: Novartis East Hanover Facility
top_10.notes: Major pharmaceutical layoffs, not full closure. Headquarters location with previous 235 layoffs in 2024. Part of company restructuring.

top_10.name: Elmwood Park Power LLC
top_10.acquirability_notes: Requires further research on current ownership and status.

top_10.name: Ahold eCommerce Sales Company Fulfillment Center
top_10.notes: Fulfillment and delivery services for Stop & Shop, shifting to store-based fulfillment

top_10.name: Henry Repeating Arms Manufacturing Plant
top_10.notes: Firearms manufacturer consolidating all operations to Wisconsin facilities. Company cited heavy manufacturing regulations in NJ as factor.

top_10.name: Chambers Cogeneration LP
top_10.notes: 285MW retired coal plant in Salem County NJ that closed May 2022. Unlike sister plant Logan which was demolished, Chambers structures remain intact. Strong transmission infrastructure, adjacent to Chemours chemical complex, confirmed battery storage redevelopment plans indicate viable grid connection and regulatory approval pathway.
top_10.acquirability_notes: Joint ownership between Starwood Energy (60%) and Atlantic Power (40%) with committed battery storage redevelopment plans but no active demolition yet
top_10.current_owner: Starwood Energy Group Global LLC (60%), Atlantic Power Corporation (40%)
top_10.redevelopment_plans: Battery energy storage facility development as part of $1 billion joint venture with Energy Management Inc to store renewable energy
top_10.partnership_path: Approach Starwood Energy Group directly as they are leading the battery storage redevelopment, or potentially work with both Starwood and Atlantic Power given joint ownership structure

top_10.name: Pedricktown Cogeneration Company LP
top_10.acquirability_notes: Cogeneration facility — may have private equity or industrial owner. Research needed.

top_10.name: B L England
top_10.acquirability_notes: Requires further research on current ownership and status.

top_10.name: PSEG Essex Generating Station
top_10.notes: Plant closed in 2022 after transitioning from coal to gas peaking facility. Major environmental contamination contributor to Diamond Alkali Superfund site on Passaic River. Located in environmental justice community with persistent air quality issues. Switch house remains but main turbine hall was demolished. Site has documented history of kerosene leaks, oil spills, and regulatory violations dating to 1970s.
top_10.acquirability_notes: Site remains owned by PSEG with extensive environmental contamination history and ongoing Superfund liabilities. Major environmental justice concerns in Ironbound neighborhood. Property appears abandoned but still under utility control with active environmental monitoring obligations.
top_10.partnership_path: Direct negotiation with PSEG would be required, but significant environmental remediation costs and Superfund site proximity make acquisition challenging. Would need extensive environmental due diligence given plant's contribution to Passaic River contamination and ongoing EPA oversight.

top_10.name: Bayonne Plant Holding LLC
top_10.acquirability_notes: Requires further research on current ownership and status.
